title: FastF1 Track-Status And Weather Join
summary: Official F1 strategy material highlights Safety Car windows and pit-loss timing as direct triggers for pit calls, while FastF1 exposes per-session `track_status`, `weather_data`, and `total_laps`.
rationale: Official F1 strategy material highlights Safety Car windows and pit-loss timing as direct triggers for pit calls, while FastF1 exposes per-session `track_status`, `weather_data`, and `total_laps`. The current best features only proxy these regimes indirectly through lap deltas and field aggregates.
implementation_hint: Join each Year/Race to the FastF1 race session and derive per-lap features: SC/VSC/yellow flags, laps since status change, restart-lap indicator, AirTemp/TrackTemp/Rainfall/WindSpeed, and exact scheduled laps remaining from `total_laps`. Interact them with compound, tyre-life ratio, and position. Validate with grouped race folds and strict name-mapping checks.
expected_effect: Should improve opportunistic next-lap pit detection in caution, restart, and weather-shift regimes, with more upside on public LB than on already-smooth local CV.
risk: External data joins can fail silently from race-name mismatches or missing session fields; older or unusual sessions may have sparse status/weather coverage.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
